2603.19997.title: When Contextual Inference Fails: Cancelability in Interactive Instruction Following
2603.19997.paper_summary: We investigate the separation of literal interpretation from contextual inference in a collaborative block-building task where a builder must resolve underspecified instructions using contextual inferences. Building on an existing two-speaker psycholinguistic paradigm -- which contrasts a pragmatically cooperative speaker with one who is only literally reliable -- we introduce Build What I Mean (BWIM), an interactive benchmark for contextual meaning construction. In BWIM, models must resolve ambiguity by either performing a contextual inference or requesting clarification at a small communication cost. Evaluating several state-of-the-art LLMs, we find a dissociation between judgment and action: while models detect speaker unreliability in explicit confidence ratings, they fail to exploit this information to guide efficient clarification behavior. Instead, we observe suboptimal strategies, such as partner-blind over-clarification and question-averse guessing under uncertainty.
2603.19997.paper_summary_zh: 我們調查了在一個合作的積木建造任務中，字面解釋與上下文推斷的分離，其中建造者必須利用上下文推斷來解決不明確的指示。基於現有的雙說話者心理語言學範式——該範式對比了一位語用上合作的說話者與一位僅在字面上可靠的說話者——我們引入了建造我所意味的（BWIM），這是一個用於上下文意義建構的互動基準。在BWIM中，模型必須通過執行上下文推斷或在小的交流成本下請求澄清來解決模糊性。評估幾種最先進的LLM時，我們發現判斷與行動之間存在分離：儘管模型在明確的信心評級中檢測到說話者的不可靠性，但它們未能利用這些信息來指導有效的澄清行為。相反，我們觀察到次優策略，例如對夥伴盲目的過度澄清和在不確定性下對問題的迴避猜測。
2603.19997.author: Natalia Bila et.al.
2603.19997.authors: Natalia Bila, Kata Naszádi, Alexandra Mayn, Christof Monz
